(French pronunciation: [[dø sɛvʁ]](/wiki/Help:IPA/French "Help:IPA/French") [ⓘ](/wiki/File:LL-Q150_(fra)-GrandCelinien-Deux-S%C3%A8vres.wav "File:LL-Q150 (fra)-GrandCelinien-Deux-Sèvres.wav"), [Poitevin-Saintongese](/wiki/Poitevin-Saintongese "Poitevin-Saintongese"): *Deùs Saevres*) is a [French department](/wiki/Departments_of_France "Departments of France"). *Deux-Sèvres* literally means "two Sèvres": the [Sèvre Nantaise](/wiki/S%C3%A8vre_Nantaise "Sèvre Nantaise") and the [Sèvre Niortaise](/wiki/S%C3%A8vre_Niortaise "Sèvre Niortaise") are two rivers which have their sources in the department. It had a population of 375,229 in 2023.[[3]](#cite_note-pophist-3)

**Deux-Sèvres** (French pronunciation: [[dø sɛvʁ]](/wiki/Help:IPA/French "Help:IPA/French") [ⓘ](/wiki/File:LL-Q150_(fra)-GrandCelinien-Deux-S%C3%A8vres.wav "File:LL-Q150 (fra)-GrandCelinien-Deux-Sèvres.wav"), [Poitevin-Saintongese](/wiki/Poitevin-Saintongese "Poitevin-Saintongese"): *Deùs Saevres*) is a [French department](/wiki/Departments_of_France "Departments of France"). *Deux-Sèvres* literally means "two Sèvres": the [Sèvre Nantaise](/wiki/S%C3%A8vre_Nantaise "Sèvre Nantaise") and the [Sèvre Niortaise](/wiki/S%C3%A8vre_Niortaise "Sèvre Niortaise") are two rivers which have their sources in the department. It had a population of 375,229 in 2023.[[3]](#cite_note-pophist-3)

## In history and literature

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Deux-S%C3%A8vres&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: In history and literature")]

*Deux-Sèvres* was one of the 83 original *départements* created during the [French Revolution](/wiki/French_Revolution "French Revolution") on 4 March 1790. The land had been part of the ancient province of [Poitou](/wiki/Poitou "Poitou").

Departmental borders were changed in 1973 when the inhabitants of the little commune of [Puy-Saint-Bonnet](https://fr.wikipedia.org/wiki/Le_Puy-Saint-Bonnet "fr:Le Puy-Saint-Bonnet") became formally [associated](/wiki/Associated_communes_of_France "Associated communes of France") with the rapidly growing adjacent commune of [Cholet](/wiki/Cholet "Cholet"), which is in the neighbouring department of [Maine-et-Loire](/wiki/Maine-et-Loire "Maine-et-Loire"). To prevent the associated communes from being administered in separate departments, Puy-Saint-Bonnet was transferred into Maine-et-Loire.

Deux-Sèvres features in *Son Excellence Eugene Rougon*, a novel by Émile Zola in his Rougon-Macquart series, when Rougon visits Niort, the departmental capital, to open a new rail line to Angers (chapter 10).

## Geography and economics

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Deux-S%C3%A8vres&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Geography and economics")]

The climate is mild, the annual temperature averaging 11 degrees Celsius.

The *département* remains rural: three-quarters of the area consists of arable land. Wheat and oats are the main products grown, as well as potatoes, apples, and walnuts. [Niort](/wiki/Niort "Niort") is the center for growing vegetables and [angelica](/wiki/Angelica "Angelica"). Some beetroot is grown in the district of [Melle](/wiki/Melle,_Deux-S%C3%A8vres "Melle, Deux-Sèvres"). Vineyards are numerous in the north ([Haut-poitou (AOVDQS)](/w/index.php?title=Haut-poitou_(AOVDQS)&action=edit&redlink=1 "Haut-poitou (AOVDQS) (page does not exist)") [[fr](https://fr.wikipedia.org/wiki/Haut-poitou_(AOVDQS) "fr:Haut-poitou (AOVDQS)")]), and there are some in the south.

The *département* is also well known for the breeding of cattle, mules, and horses. The [Parthenais](/wiki/Parthenais "Parthenais") breed of cattle is named after the town of [Parthenay](/wiki/Parthenay "Parthenay") in the north of the *département*. Also, dairy products are produced in significant quantities (butter in [Échiré](/wiki/%C3%89chir%C3%A9 "Échiré") and goats' cheese).

Some quarries are in operation (in [Mazières-en-Gâtine](/wiki/Mazi%C3%A8res-en-G%C3%A2tine "Mazières-en-Gâtine"), [La Peyratte](/wiki/La_Peyratte "La Peyratte") or [Saint-Varent](/wiki/Saint-Varent "Saint-Varent")), as well as lime extraction operations.

Textiles, leather-tanning, and flour milling were the traditional industries of Niort, the capital and major city. Nowadays, Niort, with 60,000 inhabitants, is an important commercial and administrative center. In particular it is one of the main financial centers in France (fourth in rank after Paris, Lyon and Lille). Niort is the national headquarters of some of the major insurance companies in France ([Mutuelle d'assurance des artisans de France](/w/index.php?title=Mutuelle_d%27assurance_des_artisans_de_France&action=edit&redlink=1 "Mutuelle d'assurance des artisans de France (page does not exist)") [[fr](https://fr.wikipedia.org/wiki/Mutuelle_d%27assurance_des_artisans_de_France "fr:Mutuelle d'assurance des artisans de France")] (MAAF), [Mutuelle assurance des commerçants et industriels de France et des cadres et des salariés de l'industrie et du commerce](/w/index.php?title=Mutuelle_assurance_des_commer%C3%A7ants_et_industriels_de_France_et_des_cadres_et_des_salari%C3%A9s_de_l%27industrie_et_du_commerce&action=edit&redlink=1 "Mutuelle assurance des commerçants et industriels de France et des cadres et des salariés de l'industrie et du commerce (page does not exist)") [[fr](https://fr.wikipedia.org/wiki/Mutuelle_assurance_des_commer%C3%A7ants_et_industriels_de_France_et_des_cadres_et_des_salari%C3%A9s_de_l%27industrie_et_du_commerce "fr:Mutuelle assurance des commerçants et industriels de France et des cadres et des salariés de l'industrie et du commerce")] (MACIF), [Mutuelle d'assurance des instituteurs de France](/w/index.php?title=Mutuelle_d%27assurance_des_instituteurs_de_France&action=edit&redlink=1 "Mutuelle d'assurance des instituteurs de France (page does not exist)") [[fr](https://fr.wikipedia.org/wiki/Mutuelle_d%27assurance_des_instituteurs_de_France "fr:Mutuelle d'assurance des instituteurs de France")] (MAIF) and others) and regional headquarters of others such as [Groupama](/wiki/Groupama "Groupama"). The regional headquarters of several national banks, including [Banque Populaire](/wiki/Banque_Populaire "Banque Populaire") and [Crédit Agricole](/wiki/Cr%C3%A9dit_Agricole "Crédit Agricole"), are also located there. The services sector is also heavily represented in Niort, in consulting, accounting, brokerage and software. Chemistry, pharmacy ([Boiron](/wiki/Boiron "Boiron")) and aeronautics ([Zodiac Group](/wiki/Zodiac_Group "Zodiac Group"), [Leach International](/w/index.php?title=Leach_International&action=edit&redlink=1 "Leach International (page does not exist)"))[[4]](#cite_note-4) are the main industries.

Textiles and shoe making, furnitures, mechanics, automotive ([Heuliez](/wiki/Heuliez "Heuliez") and [Irisbus](/wiki/Iveco_Bus "Iveco Bus")), chemistry ([Rhodia](/wiki/Rhodia_(company) "Rhodia (company)") in Melle), food industry and food packaging are the major industries outside of the capital.

The unemployment rate in the *département* is very low (less than 5%) especially in the north-west, where many small and medium companies are developing rapidly.

The south-west of the *département* attracts tourists with the [Marais Poitevin](/wiki/Marais_Poitevin "Marais Poitevin") natural area; the [Atlantic](/wiki/Atlantic_Ocean "Atlantic Ocean") coast is close at hand.

## Transport

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Deux-S%C3%A8vres&action=edit&section=7 "Edit section: Transport")]

Niort in the south of the *département* is connected to Paris and Bordeaux by the [A10](/wiki/A10_autoroute_(France) "A10 autoroute (France)") motorway, with Nantes by the [A83](/wiki/A83_autoroute "A83 autoroute"), with La Rochelle and Poitiers by the N11. Another important road in the north of the *département* is the [*Route nationale* 149](/wiki/Route_nationale_149 "Route nationale 149") (RN149), which runs roughly east–west from [Bressuire](/wiki/Bressuire "Bressuire") to Poitiers, passing around the outskirts of Parthenay. This section of the RN149 forms part of the [European route E62](/wiki/European_route_E62 "European route E62") from Nantes to [Genoa](/wiki/Genoa "Genoa"). The road originally ran from Nantes, but has been bypassed in various stages by the new [RN249](/w/index.php?title=RN249&action=edit&redlink=1 "RN249 (page does not exist)") dual carriageway. The old section from Nantes to Bressuire has been either renamed or downgraded to the D149.

The north and south of the *département* are connected by relatively minor roads, with the D743 and D748 linking Niort to Parthenay and Bressuire respectively, whilst the D938 connects to [Thouars](/wiki/Thouars "Thouars").[[8]](#cite_note-ign33-8)

The *département* has two railway stations on the [TGV](/wiki/TGV "TGV") route between Paris and La Rochelle (Niort and [Saint-Maixent](/wiki/Saint-Maixent "Saint-Maixent")), with a journey from Niort to Paris taking 2h15.[[9]](#cite_note-tgv-9) It is also served by several [TER Nouvelle-Aquitaine](/wiki/TER_Nouvelle-Aquitaine "TER Nouvelle-Aquitaine") regional railway routes, including a route from Poitiers via Niort to La Rochelle, a route from Niort to [Saintes](/wiki/Saintes,_Charente-Maritime "Saintes, Charente-Maritime"), and a route from [Tours](/wiki/Tours "Tours") to Thouars and Bressuire.[[10]](#cite_note-10) The *région* of Nouvelle-Aquitaine provides inter-urban bus service that connects the towns and villages of the *département*.[[11]](#cite_note-trans-11)

There are no airports with scheduled airline service within the *département*, although [Niort-Souche Airport](/w/index.php?title=Niort-Souche_Airport&action=edit&redlink=1 "Niort-Souche Airport (page does not exist)") [[fr](https://fr.wikipedia.org/wiki/A%C3%A9rodrome_de_Niort_-_Souch%C3%A9 "fr:Aérodrome de Niort - Souché")] is used for private movements. The nearest commercial airports are at Poitiers ([Poitiers - Biard Airport](/wiki/Poitiers_-_Biard_Airport "Poitiers - Biard Airport")), La Rochelle ([La Rochelle - Île de Ré Airport](/wiki/La_Rochelle_-_%C3%8Ele_de_R%C3%A9_Airport "La Rochelle - Île de Ré Airport")) and Nantes ([Nantes Atlantique Airport](/wiki/Nantes_Atlantique_Airport "Nantes Atlantique Airport")).
